name: Mad Alice's Wild Rice Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine 2/3 cup wild rice with 1 1/3 cups water. Bring to a boil, then turn the heat to simmer. Cover and cook for 45 to 55 minutes or until most of the kernels have popped.
2. Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Melt butter in saucepan and sauté onions until transparent. Gradually add flour and stir until mixture thickens slightly.
3. Add broth, salt, cooked rice, and simmer for about 5 minutes.
4. Transfer to a soup tureen and blend in cream. Place back in oven for 20 to 30 minutes.
5. Remove from base and garnish with slivered almonds and grated carrots. (Makes 6 to 7 cups. You will wish you had made a double batch.)
NOTE: I always use fresh mushrooms when making this soup, usually about 1 pound sliced. Canned condensed milk can be substituted for cream or half and half.
